content: Project Atlas status: active. Phase 2 migration in progress. The ingestion pipeline is rewritten; remaining work is the dashboard and the export job. No blockers.
Atlas ingestion pipeline notes. Batches are pulled every five minutes and deduplicated by source id before the transform stage. Backpressure is handled with a bounded queue.
Project Borealis status: blocked. Waiting on the storage engine decision before the schema can be frozen. Dashboard work is paused until then.
Borealis roadmap: ship the read-only API first, then the write path, then real-time subscriptions. The subscription work depends on the chosen storage engine.
Decision: use OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for user authentication, and short-lived bearer tokens for service-to-service authentication. Rejected session cookies because the mobile client needs token refresh.
Decision: adopt SQLite with WAL mode for the storage engine. Rejected Postgres for the appliance tier because of the single-binary deployment constraint and ARM64 support.
Decision: deploy via a single GitHub Actions workflow that builds, tests, and ships an artifact to the appliance over SSH. Rejected a container registry to keep the footprint small.
Decision: cache query results in memory with a five-minute TTL and a least-recently-used eviction policy. Rejected an external cache to avoid another moving part on the appliance.
Dana is the backend lead. Prefers small reviewable pull requests and writes the database migrations. Owns the storage engine decision and the deployment pipeline.
Ravi is the mobile lead. Drove the authentication strategy and the token refresh flow. Prefers asynchronous updates over meetings.
Team conventions: write a failing test first, keep pull requests small, and record every architectural decision with its rejected alternatives. Commit messages use the conventional commits format.
Summary of reciprocal rank fusion: combine multiple ranked result lists by summing one over k plus rank. Robust to score-scale differences between lexical and vector retrieval.
Summary of BM25: a bag-of-words ranking function that scores documents by term frequency saturation and inverse document frequency, with a length-normalization term.
Northwind engagement status: active. Delivered the onboarding workshop; next is the data migration review. Invoice for the workshop has been sent.
Contoso engagement status: stopped. The pilot ended without a renewal. Keeping the relationship warm with a quarterly check-in email.
Atlas export job: nightly cron writes a CSV snapshot to object storage and emails a download link. Failures retry three times with exponential backoff.
Borealis schema draft: entities are events with a monotonic sequence number, an actor, and a JSON payload. The schema cannot be frozen until the storage engine is chosen.
Decision: apply a small recency weight when reranking retrieval results so fresher entries surface first on ties. Rejected a hard time decay because old decisions must stay findable.
Backup policy: nightly snapshot to the NAS with grandfather-father-son retention. Restores are tested monthly. The database file is copied while the writer holds a checkpoint.
Sam handles infrastructure and the deployment pipeline runbook. Set up the GitHub Actions workflow and the SSH artifact delivery to the appliance.
Summary of PKCE: proof key for code exchange protects the OAuth authorization code flow on public clients by binding the code to a verifier the client generated.
Atlas dashboard spec: a single page showing ingestion throughput, queue depth, and the last successful export time. Refreshes every thirty seconds.
Decision: emit one structured JSON log line per request to stderr and let the host journal collect it. Rejected a metrics agent because the appliance has no spare memory.
Northwind data migration plan: export legacy records to JSON, validate against the new schema, then bulk import during a maintenance window. Dana reviews the migration scripts.
Roadmap overview: finish Atlas phase two, unblock Borealis with the storage engine decision, then start the shared authentication service. Reading queue covers retrieval ranking.
Borealis API notes: the read-only endpoints page through events by sequence number. The write path is gated behind the storage engine decision and is not yet implemented.
